title: Misplaced Mail
content:
Misplaced Mail

Mail from previous residents
Multiple authors
Cooperative fiction

Spam Folder : Sequel

-

The previous residents of the very place called home’s physical mail often plagues the current owner’s (mailbox). Whether owned or rented, whether shaped in the likeness of a dolphin or a metal bay with eighty copies or home-made of wood and adorned with a hand-carved pentacle.

Ceaseless loan forgiveness offers (luck bears, come in both names), magazines long ago unsubscribed, demented birthday cards xerox purchased and snailed express from dementia riddled aunts, political registration reminders, the solicitation (the kind paid for and [of course] legal) of all services offered and rendered.

An analog issue of mail-folk in a profession preferring shorts to pants, something avoided wholesale by the digital realm, is the assault of our limited real-estate within the space of our mailbox by supposing forces.

What are the lackadaisical to do? The mailbox-less?
